preamble: The phonetic transformation from a nasalized close-mid front unrounded vowel (e.g., /ẽ/) to an oral close front unrounded vowel (/i/) involves both denasalization and raising. This shift is a documented sound change in certain language families, where the nasal quality is lost and the vowel simultaneously moves to a higher tongue position.

examples.note: The final raising of Proto-Ersuic nasalized *ẽ to oral i occurred after an apicalization change.

sources: Yu, D. (2012). Proto-Ersuic. UC Berkeley Linguistics.
